channel.title: Personal Audio Briefing
channel.description: Personal feed generated from Zotero sources.
channel.itunes:author: Personal Audio Briefing
channel.itunes:summary: Personal feed generated from Zotero sources.

item.title: Everything Is Television: Flow, Attention, and the New Media Attractor
item.description:
A fidelity-style walkthrough of Derek Thompson’s 2025 essay arguing that major media formats are converging toward continuous short-form video “flow,” with implications for social life, attention, and political culture. The script distinguishes Thompson’s claims, his cited evidence, and clearly labeled derived inferences.

Sources (APA)

  • Thompson, D. (2025). Everything Is Television. https://www.derekthompson.org/p/why-everything-became-television

Featured Notes

  • A spooky convergence is happening in media.
  • Whether the starting point is a student directory (Facebook), radio, or an AI image generator, the end point seems to be the same: a river of short-form video.
  • In his 1974 book Television: Technology and Cultural Form, Raymond Williams: wrote that “in all communications systems before [television], the essential items were discrete.”
  • 300 hours a year to learn a new skill, or participate in their community, or have more children.
